task_yaml.task:
I have a leadership review Thursday and all I've got is a raw CSV. I need
a proper analytical workbook, not just a flat pivot table. The source is
at /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/sales_2011_q1.csv — use the
Office document skill together with the Excel dashboard skill to turn it
into something presentable. The
CSV has booked transactions plus returns, voids, and late adjustments,
and I need to slice net revenue by region against product, walk the
monthly progression month-over-month with the actual deltas so I can see
Jan to Feb to Mar momentum at a glance, spot the top and bottom
performers within each region, see voids and returns and late
adjustments cleanly isolated rather than silently rolled into the
headline number, and at least get a directional read on whether Q1
looks better or worse than the prior comparable period — or call out
that a prior-period baseline isn't in the source — so the headline
grand total has some context.

Please turn it into /tmp_workspace/results/pivot.xlsx with:
- a cleaned-data sheet that keeps the original rows and makes clear which
  rows are excluded or negative;
- a region by product pivot of net revenue where void rows are
  excluded, return rows stay negative, and late_adjustment rows count
  in their correction_month;
- grand totals for the pivot;
- a monthly momentum sheet using correction_month, with net revenue and
  month-over-month % change; and
- a chart that plots monthly net revenue and MoM % change.

Use real numeric currency and percent formats, not plain-text values.
Also export /tmp_workspace/results/reconciliation.csv with one row for
each status — booked, return, void, late_adjustment — showing row
count, gross signed revenue, excluded revenue, included net revenue, and
the rule used. Add a QA_Checks sheet to the workbook that ties the CSV
reconciliation total to the pivot grand total and flags whether the void,
return, and late-adjustment rules were applied.
